steps.title: Find the goal
steps.content: To emphasize how hot the Sun is relative to nearby stars, the answer must explicitly compare the Sun's temperature to its neighbors and show its relative ranking.

steps.title: Match to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A directly ranks the Sun with “hotter than most but not all”—showing it beats 327 of 357 nearby stars in heat—which achieves the goal of emphasizing how hot the Sun is.

The other three either omit a direct comparison (C), mention stars without comparing (B), or offer a classification contrast without temperature emphasis (D).

That matches choice A — A is correct.
